title: vaccinium uliginosum subsp. nanum
intro: Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. nanum, also known as the dwarf bilberry, is a small shrub that belongs to the heath family and is native to Arctic and subarctic regions. The leaves and berries of this plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Vaccinium uliginosum subsp. nanum include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to protect against cell damage, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The berries can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and jams, and the leaves can be made into infusions or added to salves and ointments for topical use.
